paper_title: Ice Core O2 Clumped-Isotope Measurements and Analysis

methods.description: Procedure for selecting, preparing, purifying, and measuring O2 isotopologue ratios, specifically Δ36, from ice core samples using mass spectrometry, including calibration.

steps.description: Ice core samples drilled from the Greenland Ice Sheet Project (GISP2-D core) and West Antarctic Ice Sheet Divide Ice Core (WDC06A core) were used for this study. The depths for the WDC06A and GISP2-D ice cores span 2,450-2,556 m and 1,900-1,962 m respectively, corresponding to gas ages of 18-21 ka.
steps.searchable_summary: Select ice core samples from specified cores (GISP2-D, WDC06A) and depth ranges.

steps.description: For every analysis, 90-100 g of ice core sample was used.
steps.searchable_summary: Weigh out 90-100g of the selected ice core sample for analysis.

steps.description: with an average O 2 yield of ∼70 μmol using previously described methods (Severinghaus et al., 2003;Yeung et al., 2019).
steps.searchable_summary: Extract O2 gas from the ice sample using established methods, aiming for a yield of ~70 μmol.

steps.description: Collected samples were purified cryogenically using a gas chromatograph (GC) to remove nitrogen, argon and other trace gases.
steps.searchable_summary: Purify the extracted O2 gas sample cryogenically using gas chromatography to remove contaminants.

steps.description: Subsequently, the O 2 isotopologue ratios were measured using a high-resolution Nu Instruments Perspective IS isotope ratio mass spectrometer (Yeung et al., 2016).
steps.searchable_summary: Measure O2 isotopologue ratios (e.g., 16O16O, 16O17O, 16O18O, 17O18O, 18O18O) using isotope ratio mass spectrometry.

steps.description: Clumped-isotope compositions of O 2 are described by a Δ 36 value, reported in per mil (‰) against the stochastic (random) distribution of isotopes within that sample.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate the Δ36 value from the measured O2 isotopologue ratios.

steps.description: Reported Δ 36 values are calibrated against low-pressure (<2 mbar) photochemical standards generated at a range of temperatures (−63-25°C) and also high temperature standards (800°C) generated by thermal decomposition of barium peroxide (Yeung et al., 2014).
steps.searchable_summary: Calibrate the calculated Δ36 values using data from photochemical and high-temperature standards.

methods.method_name: Statistical Analysis of Ice Core Δ36 Data
methods.description: Statistical procedures applied to the measured Δ36 values from ice cores to determine mean values, variability, confidence intervals, compare different datasets, and assess sensitivity.

steps.description: LGM-aged oxygen from the West Antarctic Ice Sheet Divide (WDC06A core) ... and Greenland Ice Sheet Drilling Project (GISP2-D core) ... ice cores has a mean Δ 36 value of 2.10 ± 0.06‰ (1σ, n = 32) and 2.09 ± 0.04‰ (1σ, n = 43), respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate the mean and standard deviation (1σ) for the Δ36 values from the WDC06A and GISP2-D ice cores.

steps.description: Bootstrap resampling of the reported LGM data from both cores yields a mean LGM tropospheric Δ 36 value of 2.10 ± 0.01‰ (95% confidence interval, CI).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform bootstrap resampling on the combined LGM Δ36 data from both WDC06A and GISP2-D cores.

steps.description: Bootstrap resampling ... yields a mean LGM tropospheric Δ 36 value of 2.10 ± 0.01‰ (95% confidence interval, CI).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate the mean and 95% confidence interval (CI) for the combined LGM tropospheric Δ36 value from the bootstrap resampling results.

steps.description: with no statistically significant difference between the two cores (p = 0.31) (See Section 2.2, Figure S1 in Supporting Information S1).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform a statistical test (e.g., t-test) to compare the mean Δ36 values between the WDC06A and GISP2-D cores.

steps.description: This mean LGM Δ 36 value is higher than PI and PD values by 0.06 ± 0.01‰ and 0.09 ± 0.02‰, respectively (95% CI; Figure 1) (Yeung et al., 2019).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate the difference between the mean LGM Δ36 value and previously published PI and PD Δ36 values, including their 95% confidence intervals.
inputs: Published PI Δ36 value and CI (Yeung et al., 2019)
Published PD Δ36 value and CI (Yeung et al., 2019)

steps.description: and is not dependent on the data rejection criteria used ( Figure S1 and Text S1 in Supporting Information S1).
steps.searchable_summary: Evaluate the sensitivity or dependence of the calculated mean LGM Δ36 value on different data rejection criteria.
inputs: Different sets of data rejection criteria
outputs: Assessment of dependence (e.g., mean values calculated with different criteria)

steps.description: ( Figure 1), ( Figure S1 in Supporting Information S1).
steps.searchable_summary: Generate plots (e.g., bar plots, scatter plots) to visualize the calculated mean Δ36 values, standard deviations, confidence intervals, comparisons, and sensitivity analysis results.
